name: FIRRTL domain declaration
description: Code should include a `domain` declaration that defines a named clock domain for use in port annotations.

name: Extmodule with Domain of port annotation
description: Code should include an `extmodule` declaration where ports are annotated with `Domain of <domainName>` to associate them with a declared domain.
snippet:
extmodule ClockGate:
  input A: Domain of ClockDomain
  output B: Domain of ClockDomain

name: Per-port domains list annotation
description: Code should include a port typed as Clock or UInt with a `domains [...]` annotation that references a domain port on the same extmodule.
snippet:
input in: Clock domains [A]
input en: UInt<1> domains [A]
input out: Clock domains [B]

name: Clock gate domain cycle pattern
description: Code should include connect statements that route a clock gate's output domain back into the input path, producing a domain-cycle that inference must detect.
snippet:
inst clockGate of ClockGate
connect clockGate.in, clock
connect clockGate.en, en
connect baz.clock, clockGate.out

name: Multiple module instances creating distinct inferred domain contexts
description: Code should include multiple independent instantiations of the same module so that domain inference produces distinct inferred domain names for each instance context.
snippet:
inst bar1 of Bar
inst bar2 of Bar
connect bar1.en, en
connect bar2.en, en

name: Domain-mode infer-all command-line option
description: Code should include a `firtool -domain-mode=infer-all` invocation to trigger aggressive domain inference that surfaces confusing diagnostic messages.
